pages.raw_text:
Deputy United States Marshal
Ide leaves tonight for Seattle. Mr..
Ide is taking Riordon and Brooks,
who are accused of sellink whisky
to Indians, before the United States
court now in session. A man nam
ed Clayton, held for similar offense,
residing at North Yakima, will be
picked up en- route by Mr.. lde.


The jury in the case of James
Brown against the Great Northern
Railway company for $4,000 dam
ages returned a verdict in favor of
The defendants.


Sheriff w. ]. Doust, although a
frifie weak from the effects of his
Illness, is again at his desk.


The county commissioners meet
today.


County Commissioner Butler is
going to spend the winter in the
city and put some one in charge OF
his Moran Prairie home.
